channel.title: Frontion News - Tier 1 Headlines
channel.description: Curated geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources worldwide

item.title: 🇪🇺🇺🇦 EU Unblocks €90 Billion Loan for Ukraine After Hungary Lifts Veto
item.description: The European Union approved a massive €90 billion loan package for Ukraine after Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban dropped his opposition following his election defeat. The loan comes as Ukraine reopens the Druzhba pipeline to Hungary, ending months of stalemate.

item.title: ⚓🇺🇸 US Navy Secretary John Phelan Leaving Immediately
item.description: Navy Secretary John Phelan is departing "effective immediately," the Pentagon announced, marking another high-ranking military leader to leave the administration amid the ongoing Iran crisis. He is being replaced by Undersecretary Hung Cao.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇲🇽 Two CIA Officers Killed in Mexico Counter-Narcotics Operation
item.description: Two Americans killed in a vehicle crash in Chihuahua, Mexico were CIA officers participating in a counter-cartel operation led by Mexican armed forces. The CIA has significantly expanded its international antidrug work under the Trump administration.

item.title: 🇯🇵⚔️ Japan Lifts Post-WWII Ban on Lethal Weapons Exports
item.description: Japan has ended its post-World War II prohibition on lethal weapons exports, allowing Japanese companies to sell arms to 17 countries. The major shift from Japan's pacifist stance comes amid heightened security concerns in the Asia-Pacific region.

item.title: 💰🇮🇷 Condom Prices Could Rise 30% Due to Iran War
item.description: Karex, the world's largest condom producer, warns prices may jump 30% as the US-Iran conflict disrupts global supply chains. The Malaysian company produces over 5 billion condoms annually and supplies major brands including Durex and Trojan.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iranians cross into Turkey just to access the internet as Iran cuts global connectivity
item.description: Iran has cut off access to the global internet, forcing some citizens to travel across the border into Turkey to make video calls and access outside communications.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Ships attacked in Strait of Hormuz as US-Iran ceasefire holds but tensions persist
item.description: Three vessels came under fire in the strategic waterway hours after Trump extended the ceasefire with Iran. Peace talks in Pakistan failed to materialize.

item.title: 🇹🇼🇨🇳 Taiwan president cancels Africa trip after China pressures countries to revoke overflight rights
item.description: Lai Ching-te abandoned his visit to Eswatini after Seychelles, Mauritius and Madagascar revoked overflight permission due to intense Chinese pressure.

item.title: 🌍 Climate change poses rising threat to democracy with extreme weather disrupting elections
item.description: At least 94 elections across 52 countries have been disrupted by climate-related impacts over the past two decades, researchers found.

item.title: 🇦🇫 Trump officials consider sending Afghans who aided US forces to Democratic Republic of Congo
item.description: Up to 1,100 Afghans who helped US forces during the war could be sent to DRC after Trump stopped the resettlement program that allowed them to apply for US residency.

item.title: 🇺🇸 Trump fires Navy Secretary John Phelan amid ongoing Iran war
item.description: Pentagon announced Navy Secretary John Phelan will leave office immediately, making him the latest in a string of senior military officials dismissed during the US-Iran conflict.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran fires on three ships in Strait of Hormuz as ceasefire holds
item.description: Iran state TV reported the Revolutionary Guard attacked two ships which are now in Iranian custody, extending the blockade of the vital waterway.

item.title: 🇬🇧 UK PM Starmer refuses to resign amid growing Mandelson scandal
item.description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer faced fresh calls to step down over his appointment of Peter Mandelson, defending his decision in Parliament.

item.title: 🇨🇳🇹🇼 US slams China after African countries block Taiwan presidents eSwatini trip
item.description: A State Department official called the incident an abuse of the international civil aviation system after African nations prevented Taiwan President Tsai Ing-wen from transiting.

item.title: ⚔️ War in Iran has depleted half of US Patriot and THAAD missile stockpiles
item.description: The prolonged conflict with Iran has consumed significant portions of American air defense reserves, raising concerns about global military readiness.

item.title: ⚓ Pentagon replaces Navy Secretary amid Iran blockade
item.description: US Navy Secretary John C Phelan is leaving "effective immediately" and being replaced by Undersecretary Hung Cao, with the Pentagon providing no explanation for the sudden leadership change during the ongoing Iran crisis.

item.title: 🇺🇸 Two Americans killed in Mexico crash were CIA officers
item.description: Two Americans killed in a vehicle crash in Chihuahua, Mexico on Sunday were CIA officers returning from a counter-cartel operation led by Mexican armed forces. The incident raises questions about US-Mexico security cooperation.

item.title: 🇲🇽 Migrant caravan leaves southern Mexico, many no longer US-bound
item.description: A migrant caravan departed from a southern Mexican city, but participants say many are no longer aiming for the US border, reflecting shifting migration patterns amid heightened enforcement.

item.title: 💰 Condom prices could surge 30% due to Iran war
item.description: Karex, the world's largest condom producer which supplies brands like Durex and Trojan, warns prices could rise 30% as the US-Iran conflict disruptpts global supply chains.

item.title: ⚖️ El Salvador holds mass trial for 486 alleged MS-13 members
item.description: El Salvador conducted a mass trial for 486 alleged members of the notorious MS-13 gang, with human rights groups warning that collective prosecutions violate due process and block defendants from accessing legal counsel.

item.title: ✈️⛽ Lufthansa Cuts 20,000 Summer Flights as Iran War Drives Jet Fuel Prices Higher
item.description: Germany's Lufthansa will eliminate 20,000 European short-haul flights this summer as jet fuel prices doubled since the US-Iran war began. The carrier is closing its CityLine service and canceling routes to 10 cities as the Strait of Hormuz disruption threatens Europe's fuel supply.

item.title: 🇹🇼🇨🇳 Taiwan President Cancels Africa Trip After China Pressures Countries to Revoke Flight Permits
item.description: President Lai Ching-te called off his visit to Eswatini after Seychelles, Mauritius, and Madagascar revoked his flight permits following what Taiwan calls "intense pressure" from Beijing. It's the first time a Taiwanese leader has had to cancel a trip due to revoked flight permits.

item.title: 🚢🇮🇷 Iran Seizes Two Cargo Ships in Strait of Hormuz as Blockade Standoff Continues
item.description: Iranian forces attacked and seized two vessels in the Strait of Hormuz, with one cargo ship reported as "heavily damaged." The escalation comes as Iran says reopening the strait is "impossible" if the US blockade continues, keeping the Gulf shipping chokepoint on edge.

item.title: 🇲🇩 Moldova's former powerful oligarch Plahotniuc sentenced to 19 years in prison
item.description: Vladimir Plahotniuc, Moldova's wealthiest businessman who de facto controlled the country's government in the 2010s, was sentenced in absentia. His fall is seen as part of Moldova's alignment with European democratic values.

item.title: 🇩🇪 Germany unveils strategy to become Europe's strongest military by 2039
item.description: The Bundeswehr will move away from rigid hardware quotas toward a flexible, effects-based planning model. The plan comes amid concerns about Russian military buildup.
